focus: persistent publication pointers and flat-artifact hydration trust boundaries

findingDetails.title: Published SQLite opens do not enforce the manifest's content digest
findingDetails.evidence: The manifest supports SHA-256 verification, but openPublishedStyleDatabase checks only the opened database's internal generation_hash.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact search found optional digest verification in the manifest helper/tests but no production open invocation; query, hydration, status, and repair share openPublishedStyleDatabase.
findingDetails.recommendation: Verify the manifest artifact digest before exposing database rows, or enforce and test an equivalent immutable publication guarantee.

adjudication.claim: A same-generation modified SQLite artifact can pass production pointer binding without matching the manifest sha256.
adjudication.counterevidenceSought: Reviewed containment, generation-mismatch, artifact-tamper, operator, retrieval, adapter, and hydration tests; 35/35 passed, but production open does not call the verifier.
adjudication.alternativeExplanation: Generation-named local artifacts may be operationally treated as immutable, but no in-scope enforcement was found.
adjudication.downgradeTrigger: Prove enforced artifact immutability or production digest verification before row exposure.

traceabilityChecks.evidence: Publication containment and generation binding are implemented, but the production open path does not enforce the recorded SQLite digest.
Dedicated traceability replay remains for iteration 3.

noveltyJustification: The first security pass identified one new cross-consumer publication-integrity gap; prior state had no findings.
edgeCases: Local artifact write access is required, so severity remains P1 rather than P0.
Generation-named immutability is described but not enforced in the reviewed surface.
Code Graph unavailable; exact search/direct-read fallback used.

focus: full spec_code and checklist_evidence replay across packet 012/008, packets 015/001/005/006, the 015 parent, and the playbook capability overlay; refine P1-001 with complete adjudication

findingDetails.title: Published SQLite opens do not enforce the manifest content digest
findingDetails.evidence: Production opens compare only the database-owned generation_hash and never invoke the manifest SHA-256 verifier.
findingDetails.scopeProof: All production query, hydration, status, and repair consumers funnel through openPublishedStyleDatabase; verifyDigests:true appears only in manifest tests.

adjudication.claim: A published SQLite artifact can be altered without changing its internal generation_hash and production opens will not compare it with the manifest sha256.
adjudication.counterevidenceSought: Re-read production open and verifier, searched consumers/call sites, and reran 69 database tests; optional tamper verification works but production opens do not invoke it.
adjudication.alternativeExplanation: Generation-named local artifacts may be operationally treated as immutable, but no in-scope read-only guarantee enforces that assumption.
adjudication.downgradeTrigger: Enforced immutable publication or production digest verification before rows are exposed.

findingDetails.title: Manual testing playbook uses removed pre-restructure paths
findingDetails.evidence: Mandatory scenarios and commands target nonexistent styles/_db and styles/_engine trees.
findingDetails.scopeProof: No files exist under either documented tree; relocated engine and database suites pass at styles/tests/engine and styles/tests/database.

adjudication.claim: The required manual release-readiness playbook points to removed pre-restructure paths, so its mandatory database and engine scenarios are not executable as written.
adjudication.counterevidenceSought: Checked for compatibility directories and found none; ran relocated suites successfully.
adjudication.alternativeExplanation: Paths could be historical shorthand, but the playbook explicitly requires those on-disk artifacts and commands.
adjudication.downgradeTrigger: An in-scope resolver or compatibility surface makes every documented command execute unchanged.

findingDetails.title: Parent phase map reports shipped children as planned
findingDetails.evidence: The parent map and next action say 001, 005, and 006 are planned/to-build while child packets report COMPLETE or IMPLEMENTED.
findingDetails.scopeProof: All three in-scope child status surfaces contradict the parent; graph metadata has no last-active-child override.

adjudication.claim: The governing phase-parent map and resume metadata contradict the shipped states of 001, 005, and 006, making current sequence and remaining work unreliable.
adjudication.counterevidenceSought: Compared parent map with each child status and checked parent graph metadata for an override; none resolves the contradiction.
adjudication.alternativeExplanation: The map may be a planning snapshot, but it is presented as current governing transition/resume state without a historical qualifier.
adjudication.downgradeTrigger: A named current parent authority is proven to supersede this map for resume, release, and remaining-work decisions.

findingDetails.title: Post-query generation drift is mislabeled as no-fit in every corpus consumer
findingDetails.evidence: All four consumers detect a post-query generation mismatch but pass no-fit into builders that already model generation-mismatch as requery-required.
findingDetails.scopeProof: All four manifest-listed production classifiers repeat the branch; 76/76 focused tests show the current no-fit behavior is stable.
findingDetails.recommendation: Pass generation-mismatch through each post-query mismatch branch and assert requery-required proof semantics.

claimAdjudication.claim: All four corpus consumers suppress their modeled requery-required state by classifying post-query generation drift as no-fit.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Mismatch warnings, no-source-influence containment, and concurrent-generation tests were reviewed; they prevent P0 stale-source use but do not emit requery-required.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: No-fit may be intended as a safe target-derived continuation.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: An explicit governing contract defines post-query mismatch as no-fit and proves no caller relies on requery-required.

findingDetails.title: Post-query generation drift is mislabeled as no-fit in every corpus consumer
findingDetails.evidence: All four consumers retain no-fit for post-query generation drift, and their green concurrent-change tests do not assert the modeled requery-required proof state.
findingDetails.scopeProof: A manifest-restricted search of 21 test files found generation-mismatch coverage but no requery-required assertion; four corpus suites passed with the current behavior.
findingDetails.recommendation: Emit generation-mismatch at all four post-query mismatch branches and assert requery-required while retaining no-source-influence containment.

claimAdjudication.claim: All four corpus consumers suppress their modeled requery-required state by classifying post-query generation drift as no-fit, and the green concurrent-change tests do not assert the required recovery contract.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Re-read the primary production mismatch branch, searched all 21 manifest-listed tests for generation-mismatch/requery-required/corpus-changing assertions, and reran the four corpus suites. Existing mismatch-plan tests prove no stale hydration, but concurrent-change tests do not require requery-required.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: No-fit could be an intentional safe continuation because the code blocks stale source influence and retains a mismatch warning.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if a governing contract explicitly defines post-query drift as no-fit and executable caller tests prove that no recovery path depends on requery-required.

focus: operator/API parity for options, defaults, exits, result fields, repair semantics, and absent-generation behavior

findingDetails.title: Operator options with missing values silently target defaults
findingDetails.evidence: A present --database without a value is replaced by the default path; malformed status returned success despite the invalid-input contract.
findingDetails.scopeProof: All options share optionValue; tests cover valued happy paths and a malformed status probe reproduced default selection.

claimAdjudication.claim: Missing option values and unknown flags are accepted and can select the default database.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Required corpus/generation/profile guards and 13/13 tests were checked; no generic missing-value or unknown-option validation exists.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Permissive parsing may be intentional and omission legitimately uses the default.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: A governing contract permits unknown flags and proves valueless path options cannot reach mutations.

findingDetails.title: Status cannot report an uninitialized clean-checkout database root
findingDetails.evidence: readdir runs before pointer absence handling, so a missing parent throws ENOENT instead of published:false.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Absent-parent probe reproduced ENOENT; the test creates its fixture directory before status.

claimAdjudication.claim: Status throws internal ENOENT when the database root has never been created.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Existing empty directories return published:false; only absent-parent status fails.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Directory creation could be an undocumented precondition.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: A governing contract requires pre-creation and bootstrap guarantees it before status.

findingDetails.title: Operator options with missing values silently target defaults
findingDetails.evidence: A present --database without a value is replaced by the default path; malformed status returned success despite the invalid-input contract.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Every option uses optionValue; direct reread found no missing-value or unknown-option validation.

claimAdjudication.claim: Missing option values and unknown flags are accepted; a trailing --database silently selects the default database despite the invalid-input failure contract.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Required corpus/generation checks exist, but no branch validates a missing database value or unknown option.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Permissive parsing may be intentional and omission legitimately uses the default.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: A governing contract permits unknown flags and proves valueless path options cannot reach state-changing commands.

findingDetails.title: Status cannot report an uninitialized clean-checkout database root
findingDetails.evidence: Status throws internal ENOENT when the database root has never been created instead of returning the modeled unpublished status.
findingDetails.scopeProof: No guard precedes readdir; the modeled unpublished branch is reached only when the directory exists.

claimAdjudication.claim: Status throws internal ENOENT when the database root has never been created instead of returning the modeled unpublished status.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Existing empty directories return published:false, but no pre-readdir guard handles a missing parent.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Directory creation could be an undocumented operator precondition.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: A governing contract requires pre-creation and an owned bootstrap guarantees it before every status call.

findingDetails.title: The 10/10 shadow result does not prove full facade DTO parity
findingDetails.evidence: Shadow parity compares a projection and tolerates score values, ranking mode, content hash, and generation hash, so 10/10 cannot prove full facade DTO parity.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The manifest has one shadow fixture assertion and a nine-scenario persistent golden oracle, but no committed ten-query legacy/persistent full-DTO artifact.

claimAdjudication.claim: The recorded 10/10 result cannot establish full facade DTO parity because the comparator projects cards and eligibility while tolerating score, ranking, content-hash, and generation differences.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Eleven focused tests prove stable persistent output, scale replay, and one fixture shadow case, but not full legacy-vs-persistent DTO equality over ten real queries.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Facade parity may intentionally mean only card projection and eligibility, with ranking internals declared non-contractual.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Packet authorities narrow the claim to projected fields, or a committed ten-query artifact proves equality for every contractually relevant facade DTO field.

findingDetails.title: The p95 1150→53 ms gate is asserted without reproducible measurement evidence
findingDetails.evidence: The p95 1150 to 53 ms claim has no manifest-listed trace, raw samples, percentile calculation, warm/cold policy, or timing harness.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The only executable latency assertion is warmed median-of-three timing for one request over 20 synthetic styles.

claimAdjudication.claim: The completed p95 1150-to-53 ms gate is not independently reproducible from the frozen manifest because no trace, samples, percentile method, warm/cold policy, or matching harness is present.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: The focused fixture benchmark confirms persistent is faster after warming, but measures a median of three for one synthetic request, not a representative-trace p95.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: The raw benchmark may have been run manually and intentionally omitted because the default cutover remains human-gated.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: A manifest-listed immutable trace and harness reproduce the stated p95 values and disclose sample, warm-up, environment, and timing-boundary methodology.

focus: correctness final adversarial stabilization/replay of all ten substantive active P1 claims and append-only reducer identity repair

findingDetails.title: Operator options with missing values silently target defaults
findingDetails.evidence: optionValue returns null for a present valueless option and the dispatcher coalesces missing --database to the default; a read-only status probe returned ok:true.
findingDetails.scopeProof: All commands share optionValue for database targeting; focused tests cover valued paths, while the malformed probe reproduced default selection.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reject present options without values and unknown options before selecting defaults; add parser matrix tests.

claimAdjudication.claim: A present operator option without its required value is accepted and may silently select the default database.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Reviewed command-specific guards and ran the operator suite; generic missing-value and unknown-option rejection remain absent.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Omitted database may intentionally default, but an explicitly present valueless flag is invalid under the documented nonzero invalid-input contract.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: A documented valueless-option contract, or generic rejection plus focused tests, disproves the claim.

findingDetails.title: Status throws when the database parent directory is absent
findingDetails.evidence: listGenerationPaths calls readdir before pointer absence handling; a read-only absent-parent probe returned ENOENT instead of published:false.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Status has one generation-directory enumerator; direct read and absent-parent probe reproduce the throw before its modeled absence DTO.
findingDetails.recommendation: Treat an absent parent as an empty generation set and add a clean-checkout status test.

claimAdjudication.claim: Status throws ENOENT instead of returning the modeled unpublished DTO when the database parent directory does not exist.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Reviewed pointer absence handling and ran the operator suite; only existing-directory behavior is protected.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Install-time setup may normally create the directory, but clean-checkout status is explicitly modeled and claimed.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: An enforced parent-directory precondition, or published:false handling plus a focused absent-parent test, disproves the claim.
